devanagari: सूत उवाच । निशम्य कौषारविणोपवर्णितं । ध्रुवस्य वैकुण्ठपदाधिरोहणम् । प्ररूढभावो भगवत्यधोक्षजे । प्रष्टुं पुनस्तं विदुरः प्रचक्रमे ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca niśamya kauṣāraviṇopavarṇitaṃ dhruvasya vaikuṇṭhapadādhirohaṇam prarūḍhabhāvo bhagavatyadhokṣaje praṣṭuṃ punastaṃ viduraḥ pracakrame

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Sūta said:
After listening to (the account of) Dhruva’s ascension to the Vaikuṇṭha region as described by Maitreya, Vidura whose devotion to Lord Viṣṇu (Adhokṣaja) was intensified, proceeded again to make further queries.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । के ते प्रचेतसो नाम कस्यापत्यानि सुव्रत । कस्यान्ववाये प्रख्याताः कुत्र वा सत्रमासत ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca ke te pracetaso nāma kasyāpatyāni suvrata kasyānvavāye prakhyātāḥ kutra vā satramāsata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Vidura said:
Oh strict observer of religious vows (Maitreya)! Who were those Pracetasas? Whose children were they? To whose familyline were they known to belong? Where did they have the sacrificial session?

devanagari: मन्ये महाभागवतं नारदं देवदर्शनम् । येन प्रोक्तः क्रियायोगः परिचर्याविधिर्हरेः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: manye mahābhāgavataṃ nāradaṃ devadarśanam yena proktaḥ kriyāyogaḥ paricaryāvidhirhareḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: I regard Nārada as a great devotee of the Lord, and as one who has directly realized the Almighty. It is he who has expounded (in the Pañcarātra Āgama) the Kriyā-Yoga (Yoga of practical devotion), in the form of the detailed procedure of worshipping Hari.

devanagari: स्वधर्मशीलैः पुरुषैः भगवान् यज्ञपूरुषः । इज्यमानो भक्तिमता नारदेनेरितः किल ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: svadharmaśīlaiḥ puruṣaiḥ bhagavān yajñapūruṣaḥ ijyamāno bhaktimatā nāradeneritaḥ kila
translation: They say that while the Lord, the presiding deity of sacrifices, was being worshipped (by performing sacrifice) by men (Pracetasas) who were devoted to their righteous duties, Nārada, who was full of devotion, extolled him.

devanagari: यास्ता देवर्षिणा तत्र वर्णिता भगवत्कथाः । मह्यं शुश्रूषवे ब्रह्मन् कार्त्स्न्येनाचष्टुमर्हसि ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yāstā devarṣiṇā tatra varṇitā bhagavatkathāḥ mahyaṃ śuśrūṣave brahman kārtsnyenācaṣṭumarhasi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb]
[accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: Oh Brahman, you should please narrate to me in details, all the stories of the Lord which have been related there, in that sacrifice by the divine sage (Nārada), as I long to hear them.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । ध्रुवस्य चोत्कलः पुत्रः पितरि प्रस्थिते वनम् । सार्वभौमश्रियं नैच्छद् अधिराजासनं पितुः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca dhruvasya cotkalaḥ putraḥ pitari prasthite vanam sārvabhaumaśriyaṃ naicchad adhirājāsanaṃ pituḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
When the father (Dhruva) retired to forest, Dhruva’s (eldest) son Utkala did not wish to ascend the imperial throne and to enjoy the imperial glory.

devanagari: स जन्मनोपशान्तात्मा निःसङ्गः समदर्शनः । ददर्श लोके विततं आत्मानं लोकमात्मनि ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa janmanopaśāntātmā niḥsaṅgaḥ samadarśanaḥ dadarśa loke vitataṃ ātmānaṃ lokamātmani

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: From his very birth, he was of tranquil mind, unattached and of impartial outlook. He saw his Soul as pervading the world and the world abiding in his Soul.

devanagari: आत्मानं ब्रह्म निर्वाणं प्रत्यस्तमितविग्रहम् । अवबोधरसैकात्म्यं आनन्दं अनुसन्ततम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānaṃ brahma nirvāṇaṃ pratyastamitavigraham avabodharasaikātmyaṃ ānandaṃ anusantatam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: By the fire of the uninterrupted practice of Yoga, Utkala had burnt down all the impurities of the past actions in his mind. He realized that the individual Self is the same as the tranquil Brahman in whom all differences dissolve and disappear, and who is the embodiment of the joy of pure knowledge, absolute bliss, existing everywhere. Knowing thus his own real Self, he ceased to perceive anything different from it.

devanagari: अव्यवच्छिन्नयोगाग्नि दग्धकर्ममलाशयः । स्वरूपमवरुन्धानो नात्मनोऽन्यं तदैक्षत ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: avyavacchinnayogāgni dagdhakarmamalāśayaḥ svarūpamavarundhāno nātmano

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [aorist middle third plural]

translation: By the fire of the uninterrupted practice of Yoga, Utkala had burnt down all the impurities of the past actions in his mind. He realized that the individual Self is the same as the tranquil Brahman in whom all differences dissolve and disappear, and who is the embodiment of the joy of pure knowledge, absolute bliss, existing everywhere. Knowing thus his own real Self, he ceased to perceive anything different from it.

devanagari: जडान्धबधिरोन्मत्त मूकाकृतिरतन्मतिः । लक्षितः पथि बालानां प्रशान्तार्चिः इवानलः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: jaḍāndhabadhironmatta mūkākṛtiratanmatiḥ lakṣitaḥ pathi bālānāṃ praśāntārciḥ ivānalaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ lakṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ lakṣ class 10 verb]

translation: To ignorant persons on the road, he (outwardly) appeared to be a stupid, blind, deaf, mad or a dumb man, though (in reality) he was mentally not like any of these. He was like a fire that had ceased to send out flames.

devanagari: मत्वा तं जडमुन्मत्तं कुलवृद्धाः समंत्रिणः । वत्सरं भूपतिं चक्रुः यवीयांसं भ्रमेः सुतम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: matvā taṃ jaḍamunmattaṃ kulavṛddhāḥ samaṃtriṇaḥ vatsaraṃ bhūpatiṃ cakruḥ yavīyāṃsaṃ bhrameḥ sutam

[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Considering him to be a dull idiot, the elders of the family along with the ministers, coronated the younger brother Vatsara, the son of Bhrami, as the ruler of the earth.

devanagari: स्वर्वीथिर्वत्सरस्येष्टा भार्यासूत षडात्मजान् । पुष्पार्णं तिग्मकेतुं च इषमूर्जं वसुं जयम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: svarvīthirvatsarasyeṣṭā bhāryāsūta ṣaḍātmajān puṣpārṇaṃ tigmaketuṃ ca iṣamūrjaṃ vasuṃ jayam

translation: Svarvīthi, the beloved wife of Vatsara, gave birth to six sons, viz., Puspārṇa. Tigmaketu, Iṣa, Ūrja, Vasu and Jaya.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां । चतुर्थस्कन्धे त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmadbhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ caturthaskandhe trayodaśo
translation: Puṣpārṇa had two wives—Prabhā (Day-light) and Doṣā (Night). Prātar (morning), Madhyandina (Mid-day) and Sāyam (Evening) were the sons of Prabhā (Day-light).

devanagari: प्रदोषो निशिथो व्युष्ट इति दोषासुतास्त्रयः । व्युष्टः सुतं पुष्करिण्यां सर्वतेजसमादधे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: pradoṣo niśitho vyuṣṭa iti doṣāsutāstrayaḥ vyuṣṭaḥ sutaṃ puṣkariṇyāṃ sarvatejasamādadhe

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Pradoṣa (night-fall or the first part of night), Niśītha (Midnight) and Vyuṣṭa (Dawn or the late part of the night) were the three sons of Doṣā (Night). Vyuṣṭa begot from (his wife) Puṣkariṇī a son called Sarvatejas.

devanagari: स चक्षुः सुतमाकूत्यां पत्‍न्यां मनुं अवाप ह । मनोरसूत महिषी विरजान्नड्वला सुतान् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cakṣuḥ sutamākūtyāṃ pat‍nyāṃ manuṃ avāpa ha manorasūta mahiṣī virajānnaḍvalā sutān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: He got from his wife Ākūti a son, Cakṣus the Manu (after Svāyambhuva Manu). Manu’s queen Naḍvalā gave birth to (twelve) sinless sons.

devanagari: पुरुं कुत्सं त्रितं द्युम्नं सत्यवन्तमृतं व्रतम् । अग्निष्टोममतीरात्रं प्रद्युम्नं शिबिमुल्मुकम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṃ kutsaṃ tritaṃ dyumnaṃ satyavantamṛtaṃ vratam agniṣṭomamatīrātraṃ pradyumnaṃ śibimulmukam
translation: (They were) Puru, Kutsa, Trita, Dyumna, Satyavat, Ṛta, Vrata, Agniṣṭoma, Atirātra, Pradyumna, Śibi and Ulmuka.

devanagari: उल्मुकोऽजनयत् पुत्रान् पुष्करिण्यां षडुत्तमान् । अङ्गं सुमनसं ख्यातिं क्रतुं अङ्गिरसं गयम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: ulmuko aṅgaṃ sumanasaṃ khyātiṃ kratuṃ aṅgirasaṃ gayam
translation: Ulmuka begot through his wife Puṣkaraṇī six excellent sons by name Aṅga, Sumanas, Khyāti, Kratu, Aṅgiras and Gaya.

devanagari: १ सुनीथाङ्गस्य या पत्‍नी सुषुवे वेनमुल्बणम् । यद्दौःशील्यात्स राजर्षिः निर्विण्णो निरगात्पुरात् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: 1 sunīthāṅgasya yā pat‍nī suṣuve venamulbaṇam yaddauḥśīlyātsa rājarṣiḥ nirviṇṇo niragātpurāt

translation: Sunīthā, the wife of Aṅga, gave birth to the strong- willed (but wicked) Vena. Disgusted with his (Vena’s) wickedness, the royal sage left his capital (and retired to the forest).

devanagari: यमङ्ग शेपुः कुपिता वाग्वज्रा मुनयः किल । गतासोस्तस्य भूयस्ते ममन्थुर्दक्षिणं करम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yamaṅga śepuḥ kupitā vāgvajrā munayaḥ kila gatāsostasya bhūyaste mamanthurdakṣiṇaṃ karam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ kup class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kup class 10 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ kup class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ kup class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kup class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: Oh child (Vidura)! The sage whose words were (destructive) like thunderbolt, got enraged and pronounced a curse on him. (As a result of the curse he fell dead). They churned again the right hand of his dead body.

devanagari: अराजके तदा लोके दस्युभिः पीडिताः प्रजाः । जातो नारायणांशेन पृथुराद्यः क्षितीश्वरः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: arājake tadā loke dasyubhiḥ pīḍitāḥ prajāḥ jāto nārāyaṇāṃśena pṛthurādyaḥ kṣitīśvaraḥ

[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: When there was no King, in that period, the subjects in the world, were troubled by robbers. Then Pṛthu, the first Lord of the earth (so called as he was the first founder of cities, habitations etc.) was born embodying in him an aṃśa or portion of Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । तस्य शीलनिधेः साधोः ब्रह्मण्यस्य महात्मनः । राज्ञः कथमभूद् दुष्टा प्रजा यद्विमना ययौ ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca tasya śīlanidheḥ sādhoḥ brahmaṇyasya mahātmanaḥ rājñaḥ kathamabhūd duṣṭā prajā yadvimanā yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Vidura said:
How is it that the high-souled, pious king, who was a treasure-house of virtues and so friendly to Brāhmaṇas got such wicked progeny, that he had to leave his capital in sadness and grief.

devanagari: किं वांहो वेन उद्दिश्य ब्रह्मदण्डं अयूयुजन् । दण्डव्रतधरे राज्ञि मुनयो धर्मकोविदाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ vāṃho vena uddiśya brahmadaṇḍaṃ ayūyujan daṇḍavratadhare rājñi munayo dharmakovidāḥ
translation: Or for what offence did the sages, who were well- versed in Dharma-Śāstra, directed their curse against King Vena, who had vowed to punish the evil-doers.

devanagari: नावध्येयः प्रजापालः प्रजाभिरघवानपि । यदसौ लोकपालानां बिभर्त्योजः स्वतेजसा ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nāvadhyeyaḥ prajāpālaḥ prajābhiraghavānapi yadasau lokapālānāṃ bibhartyojaḥ svatejasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: As the king, in the capacity of the protector of his subjects, possesses the energy and the splendour of (Indra and other) world-protectors, in his own superior glory, he should not be disrespected by the subjects, even though he be at fault.

devanagari: एतदाख्याहि मे ब्रह्मन् सुनीथात्मज चेष्टितम् । श्रद्दधानाय भक्ताय त्वं परावरवित्तमः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: etadākhyāhi me brahman sunīthātmaja ceṣṭitam śraddadhānāya bhaktāya tvaṃ parāvaravittamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ceṣṭ ], [accusative single from √ ceṣṭ ]

translation: Oh Brahman, as you are the foremost among the knowers of para and avara[1], please narrate to me, a devout devotee, the deeds of the son of Sunīthā.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
para and avara: Variously interpreted as ‘past and future’. Parmātaman [Paramātman?] and products of Prakṛti, etc.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । अङ्गोऽश्वमेधं राजर्षिः आजहार महाक्रतुम् । नाजग्मुर्देवतास्तस्मिन् आहूता ब्रह्मवादिभिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca aṅgo nājagmurdevatāstasmin āhūtā brahmavādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
The royal sage Aṅga performed a great Horse- Sacrifice (Aśvamedha). But in that sacrifice, the deities though invoked by the priests well-versed in the Vedas, did not turn up (to receive their share of the sacrificial offerings).

devanagari: तं ऊचुः विस्मितास्तत्र यजमानमथर्त्विजः । हवींषि हूयमानानि न ते गृह्णन्ति देवताः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ ūcuḥ vismitāstatra yajamānamathartvijaḥ havīṃṣi hūyamānāni na te gṛhṇanti devatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yaj class 1 verb]

[nominative plural from √ hū class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hū class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hū class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation: Then the sacrificial priests who were wonder-struck told the sacrificer (King Aṅga), “the deities do not accept the sacrificial oblations though poured into the fire.”

devanagari: राजन्हवींष्यदुष्टानि श्रद्धयाऽसादितानि ते । छन्दांस्ययातयामानि योजितानि धृतव्रतैः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: rājanhavīṃṣyaduṣṭāni śraddhayā chandāṃsyayātayāmāni yojitāni dhṛtavrataiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ yuj ], [vocative plural from √ yuj ], [accusative plural from √ yuj ]

translation: “Oh King, the materials for oblations are unpolluted and pure and have been devoutly procured by you. The Vedic incantations are not inefficacious, since they are chanted and used by persons of steadfast vows.

devanagari: न विदामेह देवानां हेलनं वयमण्वपि । यन्न गृह्णन्ति भागान् स्वान् ये देवाः कर्मसाक्षिणः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: na vidāmeha devānāṃ helanaṃ vayamaṇvapi yanna gṛhṇanti bhāgān svān ye devāḥ karmasākṣiṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation: We do not know if the slightest disregard is shown to gods here (in this performance of sacrifice) as a result of which gods who witness the performance of this sacrificial act, do not partake of their share of oblations”.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । अङ्गो द्विजवचः श्रुत्वा यजमानः सुदुर्मनाः । तत्प्रष्टुं व्यसृजद् वाचं सदस्यान् तदनुज्ञया ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca aṅgo dvijavacaḥ śrutvā yajamānaḥ sudurmanāḥ tatpraṣṭuṃ vyasṛjad vācaṃ sadasyān tadanujñayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Hearing the words of Brāhmaṇas, the host, the performer of the sacrifices, Aṅga became deeply grieved. With the permission of the members of the assembly, he broke his silence in order to ask them the reason of the non-acceptance of oblations.

devanagari: नागच्छन्त्याहुता देवा न गृह्णन्ति ग्रहानिह । सदसस्पतयो ब्रूत किमवद्यं मया कृतम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: nāgacchantyāhutā devā na gṛhṇanti grahāniha sadasaspatayo brūta kimavadyaṃ mayā kṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]
[nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]
[accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: “Oh Members of the sacrificial assembly! Here (in this sacrifice) deities even though invoked, do not attend. Nor do they accept their cups of Soma. Be pleased to tell me what censurable act has been committed by me.”

devanagari: सदसस्पतय ऊचुः । नरदेवेह भवतो नाघं तावन् मनाक् स्थितम् । अस्त्येकं प्राक्तनमघं यदिहेदृक् त्वमप्रजः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: sadasaspataya ūcuḥ naradeveha bhavato nāghaṃ tāvan manāk sthitam astyekaṃ prāktanamaghaṃ yadihedṛk tvamaprajaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation:
The Leaders of the Assembly spoke:
Oh King, there is not the slightest sin committed by you so far in this life. There is however one sin of your previous life on account of which you are thus issueless.

devanagari: तथा साधय भद्रं ते आत्मानं सुप्रजं नृप । इष्टस्ते पुत्रकामस्य पुत्रं दास्यति यज्ञभुक् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā sādhaya bhadraṃ te ātmānaṃ suprajaṃ nṛpa iṣṭaste putrakāmasya putraṃ dāsyati yajñabhuk
translation: [2] Oh King, the Lord, the enjoyer of sacrifices, when worshipped, will vouchsafe a son to you who are so desirous to have one. Try that way to have good progeny. May God bless you.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
With a view to ensuring that deities should be pleased to accept oblations from you, you should try to secure a child to you.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: तथा स्वभागधेयानि ग्रहीष्यन्ति दिवौकसः । यद् यज्ञपुरुषः साक्षाद् अपत्याय हरिर्वृतः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā svabhāgadheyāni grahīṣyanti divaukasaḥ yad yajñapuruṣaḥ sākṣād apatyāya harirvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: If Lord Hari, the presiding god of the sacrifices himself, is directly invoked by you for a child, all sacrificial deities (the residents of heaven) will automatically accept their respective share of oblations.

devanagari: तांस्तान् कामान् हरिर्दद्याद्यान् यान् कामयते जनः । आराधितो यथैवैष तथा पुंसां फलोदयः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃstān kāmān harirdadyādyān yān kāmayate janaḥ ārādhito yathaivaiṣa tathā puṃsāṃ phalodayaḥ
translation: Hari confers upon men (his devotees) whatever desires are cherished (and sought) by them. The attainment of the desired objects, however, depends upon the manner (and devoutness) of the worship (of the Lord)

devanagari: इति व्यवसिता विप्राः तस्य राज्ञः प्रजातये । पुरोडाशं निरवपन् शिपिविष्टाय विष्णवे ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vyavasitā viprāḥ tasya rājñaḥ prajātaye puroḍāśaṃ niravapan śipiviṣṭāya viṣṇave
translation: Thus resolved, the Brāhmaṇas offered puroḍāśa [3] to Viṣṇu in the form of Śipiviṣṭa[4], for securing progeny to that king.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Puroḍāsa— a sacrificial oblation made of ground rice and offered in a kapāla or vessel. ASD. 341.

Footnote [4]:
Śipiviṣṭa (i) ‘One who has entered the sacrificial animal (the beast to be sacrificed) in the form of Sacrifice’. Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a Śruti which means that Viṣṇu resides in the sacrificial animal as the spirit of the sacrifice. This form of Viṣṇu is invoked in the sacrifice specifically performed to get a son. (Putrakāmeṣṭi).
(ii) Or: from whom rays are emanating—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: तस्मात्पुरुष उत्तस्थौ हेममाल्यमलाम्बरः । हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण सिद्धमादाय पायसम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātpuruṣa uttasthau hemamālyamalāmbaraḥ hiraṇmayena pātreṇa siddhamādāya pāyasam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ud class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ ud class 7 verb]
[nominative single from √ und class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ und class 7 verb]
[nominative single from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sidh class 1 verb]

translation: From that sacrifice arose a person adorned with gold necklace and clad in immaculate garments, bringing with him in a gold vessel ready made pāy as a (rice boiled in milk).

devanagari: स विप्रानुमतो राजा गृहीत्वाञ्जलिनौदनम् । अवघ्राय मुदा युक्तः प्रादात्पत्‍न्या उदारधीः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa viprānumato rājā gṛhītvāñjalinaudanam avaghrāya mudā yuktaḥ prādātpat‍nyā udāradhīḥ
translation: With the assent of the Brāhmaṇas the generous- minded King accepted the boiled rice in the hollow of his folded palms, smelt it and gave it to the queen joyfully.

devanagari: सा तत्पुंसवनं राज्ञी प्राश्य वै पत्युरादधे । गर्भं काल उपावृत्ते कुमारं सुषुवेऽप्रजा ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tatpuṃsavanaṃ rājñī prāśya vai patyurādadhe garbhaṃ kāla upāvṛtte kumāraṃ suṣuve

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Having eaten that food which had the efficacy of creating a male child, the issueless queen varīly became pregnant from her husband, and in due course of time gave birth to a son.

devanagari: स बाल एव पुरुषो मातामहमनुव्रतः । अधर्मांशोद्भवं मृत्युं तेनाभवद् अधार्मिकः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bāla eva puruṣo mātāmahamanuvrataḥ adharmāṃśodbhavaṃ mṛtyuṃ tenābhavad adhārmikaḥ
translation: Since his childhood, the boy was attached to his maternal grandfather Mṛtyu who inherited a portion of Adharma. Hence he became unrighteous.

devanagari: स शरासनमुद्यम्य मृगयुर्वनगोचरः । हन्त्यसाधुर्मृगान् दीनान् वेनोऽसावित्यरौज्जनः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: sa śarāsanamudyamya mṛgayurvanagocaraḥ hantyasādhurmṛgān dīnān veno
translation: Taking up his bow and wandering as a hunter in the jungle, this wicked boy killed innocent deer. Hence people (after seeing him) would cry out “This is Vena”[5] (the tormentor).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā derives the name from ven—‘to harass’ or ‘to make others full of anxiety.’

devanagari: आक्रीडे क्रीडतो बालान् वयस्यान् अतिदारुणः । प्रसह्य निरनुक्रोशः पशुमारममारयत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: ākrīḍe krīḍato bālān vayasyān atidāruṇaḥ prasahya niranukrośaḥ paśumāramamārayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]

translation: This extremely ruthless, hard hearted boy used to slay violently like beasts, boys of his own age playing on the playground.

devanagari: तं विचक्ष्य खलं पुत्रं शासनैः विविधैर्नृपः । यदा न शासितुं कल्पो भृशं आसीत् सुदुर्मनाः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vicakṣya khalaṃ putraṃ śāsanaiḥ vividhairnṛpaḥ yadā na śāsituṃ kalpo bhṛśaṃ āsīt sudurmanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Seeing that his son was so wicked, and finding that he (the king) was not successful in disciplining him in spite of different kinds of punishments, the king became sorely dejected at heart.

devanagari: प्रायेणाभ्यर्चितो देवो येऽप्रजा गृहमेधिनः । कदपत्यभृतं दुःखं ये न विन्दन्ति दुर्भरम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: prāyeṇābhyarcito devo ye kadapatyabhṛtaṃ duḥkhaṃ ye na vindanti durbharam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vid class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: (He thought) ‘Most probably the householders who are issueless must have worshipped god for they are spared the unbearable agony caused by a wicked chjld.

devanagari: यतः पापीयसी कीर्तिः अधर्मश्च महान् नृणाम् । यतो विरोधः सर्वेषां यत आधिरनन्तकः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: yataḥ pāpīyasī kīrtiḥ adharmaśca mahān nṛṇām yato virodhaḥ sarveṣāṃ yata ādhiranantakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Indeed what wise man will think highly of the embarrassing bondage called (his own) son if he brings evil reputation (to his father) among men and implicates him in serious unrighteousness, if he creates conflicts with all and causes unending mental torture (to his father) making his home-life full of distress.

devanagari: कस्तं प्रजापदेशं वै मोहबन्धनमात्मनः । पण्डितो बहु मन्येत यदर्थाः क्लेशदा गृहाः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: kastaṃ prajāpadeśaṃ vai mohabandhanamātmanaḥ paṇḍito bahu manyeta yadarthāḥ kleśadā gṛhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kas class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kas class 1 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ paṇḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ paṇḍ class 10 verb]
[optative middle third single], [optative passive third single]

translation: Indeed what wise man will think highly of the embarrassing bondage called (his own) son if he brings evil reputation (to his father) among men and implicates him in serious unrighteousness, if he creates conflicts with all and causes unending mental torture (to his father) making his home-life full of distress.

devanagari: कदपत्यं वरं मन्ये सदपत्याच्छुचां पदात् । निर्विद्येत गृहान्मर्त्यो यत्क्लेशनिवहा गृहाः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: kadapatyaṃ varaṃ manye sadapatyācchucāṃ padāt nirvidyeta gṛhānmartyo yatkleśanivahā gṛhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ kleś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kleś class 1 verb]

translation: (In away) I regard a bad child preferable to a good child as it is a cause of many sorrows. And a man gets disgusted with his home which is full of troubles.

devanagari: एवं स निर्विण्णमना नृपो गृहात् । निशीथ उत्थाय महोदयोदयात् । अलब्धनिद्रोऽनुपलक्षितो नृभिः । हित्वा गतो वेनसुवं प्रसुप्ताम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sa nirviṇṇamanā nṛpo gṛhāt niśītha utthāya mahodayodayāt alabdhanidro hitvā gato venasuvaṃ prasuptām
translation: The king who was thus dejected at heart, could not get. any sleep. At midnight he got up and leaving Vena’s mother asleep[6] and unnoticed by other men, he departed from the palace abounding in prosperity.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā thinks that the king disguised himself while Vena’s mother was sleeping.

devanagari: विज्ञाय निर्विद्य गतं पतिं प्रजाः । पुरोहितामात्यसुहृद्‍गणादयः । विचिक्युरुर्व्यामतिशोककातरा । यथा निगूढं पुरुषं कुयोगिनः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: vijñāya nirvidya gataṃ patiṃ prajāḥ purohitāmātyasuhṛd‍gaṇādayaḥ vicikyururvyāmatiśokakātarā yathā nigūḍhaṃ puruṣaṃ kuyoginaḥ
translation: When the subjects, royal priests, ministers, friends and relatives of the king learnt that the king had departed in disgust, they became overwhelmed with excessive grief. They searched for him all over the world (without success), just as Yogins on the wrong path do in search of the unmanifest Supreme Man hidden in their heart[7].
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā states that the king was somewhere in the town while they were searching for him outside all over the world.

devanagari: अलक्षयन्तः पदवीं प्रजापतेः । हतोद्यमाः प्रत्युपसृत्य ते पुरीम् । ऋषीन् समेतान् अभिवन्द्य साश्रवो । न्यवेदयन् पौरव भर्तृविप्लवम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: alakṣayantaḥ padavīṃ prajāpateḥ hatodyamāḥ pratyupasṛtya te purīm ṛṣīn sametān abhivandya sāśravo nyavedayan paurava bhartṛviplavam
translation:
Being unable to trace any trail of the king and having thus failed in their attempts to do so, the subjects returned to the capital. Bowing to assembled sages, they reported with tearful eyes the disappearance of their king, oh Vidura (discendant of Puru).
